(d) According to the question, the direction diagram is as follows
[Figure shows path with points A, B, C, D, E where:
A = Original position, E = Finishing point
BC = 20, AB = 15m, AC = ED = 5m, CD = AE = 10m
Clearly, at finishing point E, Anoop is 10m East from original position A.

(b) According to the question, the direction diagram will be as follows
[Figure shows path from Angne to Chimur to Rewa to Rahate with distances:
Angne to Chimur: 12 km
Chimur to Rewa: 20 km
Rewa to Rahate: 18 km]
Clearly, Sanjay will go North-West starting from Rahate to reach Angne.

(d) According to the question, the direction diagram is as follows
[Figure shows path with points A, B, C, D, E where:
AB = CD = 35m
BC = AD = 20m
DE = 20m]
∴ Required distance, AE = AD + DE = 20 + 20 = 40m

(b) According to the question, the direction diagram is as follows
[Figure shows path with multiple points and directions]
Clearly, the rat is facing North at finishing point.

(c) According to the question, the direction diagram is as follows
[Figure shows path with measurements:
S = Starting point, T = Finishing point
AS = BC = 25m
AB = SC = 50m
CT = 60m]
∴ Required distance, ST = CT - SC = 60 - 50 = 10m
Clearly, at point T, Mahesh is 10m West from S.

(b) [Figure shows geometric path with points A, B, C, D]
Required distance = OD = OA + AD = OA + BC (∵ AD = BC) = 30 + 20 = 50m

(c) [Figure shows path with distances: 25km, 150km, 15km, 35km, 25km]
∴ Required distance = PQ = 150 - (25 + 25 + 35) = 65km

(c) According to the question, direction diagram is as follows
[Figure shows detailed path with measurements:
P = Starting point
S = Finishing point
PS = QR = 10m
TQ = 20m
TP = 15m
PQ = SR = 5m]
Now, it is clear from the diagram,
Required distance, PS = 10m
Clearly, at finishing point S, Ravi is 10m East from starting point P.
